title: Slot-collection interrupt — caller answers provider_type AND asks for something this agent does not own
defect: Same context-retention defect, earlier in the call: a fresh in-scope request bundled with a slot answer is dropped.
source: Phase 0 multi-intent surface (proves the defect is conversation-wide, not delivery-only)
summary: provider_search asks 'what type of provider?'. The member answers (pediatrician) and,
in the same utterance, asks a benefits question (deductible) that provider_search does
not own. WorkerResult carries only provider_type, so the benefits question is invisible.
The agent advances to ZIP confirmation and the in-scope side request is never handed

messages.content: What type of provider are you looking for?

turns.user: I'm looking for a pediatrician — also, what's my deductible?
turns.note: The deductible question (owned by benefits_agent) is not representable in WorkerResult.
turns.expected_behavior_target: Capture provider_type=Pediatrician AND queue/answer the deductible question.
turns.actual_behavior_today: Captures provider_type, advances to ZIP confirmation, never mentions the deductible.

resolved_failures.description: A fresh in-scope request bundled with a slot answer was dropped — no acknowledgement, no hand-off.
resolved_failures.assertion: The slot answer is accepted (provider_type=Pediatrician), the benefits question is acknowledged (multi_intent_ack) and parked (intent_queue contains benefits_agent), and dropped_request_count == 0.
